_purpose: Workstream A PROVE round (2026-06-09/10) — overreacted blog demo clone + honest verification of the >600-char capture fix.
verdict: FIX DOES NOT ENGAGE ON THIS ARTICLE — demo vs control textCoverage 0.413 vs 0.415 (within noise); both captures byte-identical (499 text-leaves / 35742 chars, ensemble variance 0). The >600-char keep only fires when leaf() sees a monolithic >600-char element; on today's overreacted.io every >600 run is a <pre> code block (kept by both paths) and prose paragraphs are <600 chars.
realDefectFound: capture-layout.mjs:1536 inlineSimple — a paragraph is only leafed whole if every visible inline child is svg|span|b|i|em|strong|br. Blog paragraphs with <a>/<code> children (most of overreacted's prose) fail the gate, walk() recurses, the <a>/<code> leaves are emitted and the paragraph's BARE TEXT NODES are silently dropped. LOOK: intro paragraphs render as floating pink links + code spans with the surrounding prose missing; plain paragraphs (no inline children) reproduce fully. This — not the >600 drop — is the dominant blog-body text gap (capture stats: capturedTexts 343 / domVisibleTexts 3371).
lookNotes: Fresh-load top: title + date + question list + plain paragraphs present; 'Pay what you like' pill renders EMPTY (white text on lost gradient); intro prose missing around its links. Middle: dark syntax-highlighted code blocks reproduce very well; mixed-inline paragraphs reduced to fragments. Page height matches source exactly (53761px, hRatio 1.0). Note: after a programmatic full scroll cycle the top region re-renders degraded (title/list vanish) — screenshot fresh-load for LOOK.
domProbe: Rendered clone (440 elementor text-editor widgets): FOUND 'swimming against the tide…', 'Destructuring props makes this more obvious', 'If each of these values is the same…' (full plain paragraphs, native selectable text). MISSING 'You wrote a few components with Hooks', 'class lifecycle methods' (mixed-inline paragraphs).
